what_it_does: Defines a standalone HTML preview card for a button component set, including inline CSS for variants, states, sizes, icons, and example rows.
observations: Uses dsCard metadata for Brand / Buttons with a 700x280 viewport hint.
Imports _card.css and relies on shared design tokens such as --font-body, --accent-gold, --bg-dark, --border-strong, and radius/easing variables.
Defines five button variants: primary, outline, dashed, ghost, and dark.
Includes hover, active, focus-visible, disabled, small, and large state styling.
Uses layered gradients, inset highlights, soft shadows, and transparent/tinted backgrounds to create depth.
Contains inline SVG icons for create and filter examples plus a small dot indicator for the dark menu button.
The visible demo groups examples by labeled rows: Primary, Outline, Dashed, Ghost / Dark, and Sizes.
standouts: The primary and dark variants use layered backgrounds plus multiple shadow layers for tactile depth.
Focus styling is explicitly present with a warm gold ring.
The demo shows both semantic variants and sizing/disabled examples in the same compact card.
conventions: Uses class-based CSS variants with a shared .btn base class.
Keeps component styling file-local inside a style tag for preview portability.
Uses design-system CSS variables instead of hard-coded values for recurring palette, typography, radius, and easing choices.
Groups visual examples with uppercase row labels and flex-wrapped controls.
Uses native button elements for all rendered controls.
concerns: Inline SVG icons have no accessible labels, though the button text gives context in these examples.
Disabled styling keeps hover selectors active for box-shadow changes inherited from variant rules except transform suppression.
The preview depends on external token definitions from _card.css, so this file alone does not define the full visual palette.
notes: A compact design-system preview for warm, paper-tinted button variants with stateful interaction styling.
questions: Are the buttons intended only as visual examples, or should they be connected to interaction behavior in the preview environment?
Should icon-only or aria-specific button cases exist elsewhere, since this file only demonstrates text-bearing buttons?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated Buttons preview purpose matches the file: it demonstrates softer fills, warm shadows, inset highlights, variants, states, and sizes.
intention_signals: The file seems intended to document and visually validate a button language where gold is used selectively, secondary actions stay paper-like, and interaction states feel soft rather than stark.
novelty: A button preview that combines paper-tinted fills, warm gold emphasis, inset highlight layers, and dashed placeholder-style actions within one compact component card.
contribution: Contributes a button substrate with shared base styling, variant treatments, interaction states, sizing examples, disabled behavior, compact row presentation, and a warm paper/gold visual vocabulary that a fused system could retain as component guidance.
summary_for_embedding: Standalone HTML/CSS preview card demonstrating ConceptV button variants, states, sizes, icons, and warm paper/gold styling.
intention_for_embedding: This was for presenting a branded button system with soft depth, tokenized styling, and examples of primary, outline, dashed, ghost, dark, sized, and disabled controls.
